steps.title: Set off the date phrase
steps.content: The phrase ratified in 1804 gives extra information about the 12th amendment. Because it interrupts the main clause, it should be enclosed with commas before the sentence continues to the verb separated.

steps.title: Choose the closing comma
steps.content: Choice A leaves only a dash and no date. Choice C uses a colon where the sentence needs a date and closing punctuation. Choice D includes the date but fails to close the interrupting phrase. The date followed by a comma fits. The answer is B.
